patches.summary: Drop 'Digg AI' / 'Digg AI 1000' / '1,000 AI accounts' product branding from prose surfaces; the CLI is now called 'Digg'.
patches.reason: Upstream Digg's AI-news leaderboard now markets itself as plain 'Digg' (with /ai/ as the URL prefix). The 'AI 1000' framing was inherited from an earlier iteration of the product. Keeping it in display strings was confusing users.
patches.validated_outcome: agent-context, --help, and SKILL.md frontmatter all now read 'Digg' and reference the four GitHub feeds.
patches.scope_note: Internal code identifiers (roster_1000.go, the /ai/1000 endpoint path, ai_1000_fixture.html) are left alone — they describe the upstream endpoint, not user-facing prose.

patches.summary: Add a `github` parent command with four subcommands (stars, new, activity, recent) backed by RSC parsers for the /ai/github/* pages.
patches.reason: Digg surfaces a parallel set of GitHub feeds alongside the X-account leaderboard. The original CLI shipped before those existed; users wanted them exposed as structured JSON.
patches.validated_outcome: go build / go vet / go test all green; each of the four commands returns live structured records when dogfooded (stars: nested {repo,judgment}; new: flat event records; activity: contributor leaderboard with rank/contributions/repos; recent: per-user events paired with github.com URLs).
